rationale: The proposed homestead exemption increase under Tax Code §11.13(b) concerns property tax appraisal amounts and has no bearing on parental rights governed by Educ. Code §1.009. The statutes address unrelated subject matters.
quote: The fundamental rights granted to parents by their Creator and upheld by the United States Constitution, the Texas Constitution, and the laws of this state, including the right to direct the moral and religious training of the parent's child, make decisions concerning the child's education, and consent to medical, psychiatric, and psychological treatment of the parent's child under Section 151.001, Family Code, may not be infringed on by any public elementary or secondary school or state governmental entity, including the state or a political subdivision of the state, unless the infringement is:
_note: No conflict. The proposed change is a property tax exemption adjustment; the rule governs parental rights in education and child welfare. Disjoint subject matters.
